task_completion.description: Open the standard Visitor badge template in Jolly Lobby Track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jolly Lobby Track application is open, and the badge design canvas displays a standard 'Visitor' badge template.

task_completion.description: Add a barcode element to the badge template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A barcode object is visible on the badge design canvas.

task_completion.description: Position the barcode at the bottom of the badge.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The barcode object is placed in the lower section (bottom edge or near bottom) of the badge layout.

task_completion.description: Configure the barcode to encode the dynamic 'Visitor ID' field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties or configuration dialog for the barcode is open, showing the data source, value, or binding set to the dynamic 'Visitor ID' field (e.g., selected from a database field list or using a placeholder like '[Visitor ID]').
The agent clicks the 'Save' icon, uses the File -> Save menu, or a status indicator shows the template has been successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Jolly Lobby Track GUI rather than using terminal commands or editing underlying files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Jolly Lobby Track graphical user interface being actively used (e.g., mouse cursors clicking menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes).

integrity.description: The agent used the software's built-in barcode tool rather than pasting a static image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The barcode is selected from the application's design toolbar/palette and has editable properties, rather than being an imported static image file.

integrity.description: The agent properly bound the barcode to a dynamic field rather than typing static text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The barcode properties show a data binding or variable selection for 'Visitor ID' rather than static text simply typed into a fixed value field.
